Data breach at insurance agency (60 users, cloud-heavy). Data type: CUI (controlled unclassified information). Estimated volume: 3.5 million records. Attack vector: misconfigured S3 bucket.

Ransomware (BlackCat/ALPHV) detected at nonprofit (25 users, remote-first). Entry vector: drive-by download from watering hole site. Primary target: SCADA workstation. Current impact: double extortion — data exfiltration confirmed. Ransom demand: $500,000 in Monero.

scenarios.time_context: Tuesday 2:30 PM — middle of business day
scenarios.description: Ransomware (8Base) detected at MSP client — managed 24/7 (120 endpoints). Entry vector: exploited PrintNightmare vulnerability. Primary target: ERP system. Current impact: patient records inaccessible. Ransom demand: $1.2M with 72-hour countdown.

scenarios.time_context: Friday 4:55 PM — end of week, skeleton crew
scenarios.description: Ransomware (Black Basta) detected at school district (500 users, 12 campuses). Entry vector: exploited PrintNightmare vulnerability. Primary target: RDS session host. Current impact: double extortion — data exfiltration confirmed. Ransom demand: demand doubles after 3 days.

scenarios.environment: CPA firm during tax season (50 users, sensitive data)
scenarios.description: Phishing incident at CPA firm during tax season (50 users, sensitive data). Pretext: IT helpdesk ticket resolution link. User action: user reported without clicking — early detection. Result: M365 credentials harvested via EvilProxy.

scenarios.time_context: Friday 4:55 PM — end of week, skeleton crew
scenarios.description: Phishing incident at regional bank (150 users, multiple branches). Pretext: DocuSign signature request for fake contract. User action: user downloaded and ran .exe disguised as PDF. Result: Emotet dropper installed from macro.

scenarios.environment: CPA firm during tax season (50 users, sensitive data)
scenarios.time_context: Saturday 3:00 AM — weekend, no IT staff on-site
scenarios.description: Data breach at CPA firm during tax season (50 users, sensitive data). Data type: CUI (controlled unclassified information). Estimated volume: 500,000 records. Attack vector: third-party vendor data breach.

scenarios.time_context: Friday 4:55 PM — end of week, skeleton crew
scenarios.description: Insider threat at trucking/logistics company (75 users, GPS/IoT). Actor: finance employee under financial stress. Action detected: USB drive data transfer detected by DLP. Data at risk: HR records including compensation data.
Data breach at school district (500 users, 12 campuses). Data type: customer email addresses and purchase history. Estimated volume: 125,000 records. Attack vector: insider copied to personal device.

scenarios.time_context: Tuesday 2:30 PM — middle of business day
scenarios.description: Supply chain compromise affecting county government (300 users, public services). Vector: hardware implant discovered in network equipment. Affected vendor type: accounting software provider.

scenarios.time_context: Tuesday 2:30 PM — middle of business day
scenarios.description: Ransomware (Rhysida) detected at engineering firm (90 users, IP-heavy). Entry vector: exposed RDP on port 3389. Primary target: SQL database server. Current impact: Active Directory compromised. Ransom demand: $1.2M with 72-hour countdown.

Phishing incident at retail chain (200 users, 15 POS locations). Pretext: Microsoft 365 MFA prompt bombing. User action: user clicked link and entered credentials. Result: InfoStealer exfiltrated browser passwords and cookies.
Data breach at MSP client — managed 24/7 (120 endpoints). Data type: board meeting minutes. Estimated volume: unknown — still assessing scope. Attack vector: SQL injection on public-facing app.
Unauthorized access at veterinary clinic chain (30 users, 4 locations). Method: API authentication bypass. Discovery: honeypot triggered in DMZ.
